comment:
Windows 3.11 Standard Mode — oxide86 debug session

Goal: Windows 3.11 launches and runs in Standard Mode (286 PM via DOSX.EXE).

Mechanism: Windows 3.x Standard Mode uses 286 protected mode (not 386 Enhanced Mode).
DOSX.EXE is the DOS Protected Mode Interface (DPMI) host for Standard Mode. It runs
partly at real-mode segment 0x0038 (physical base 0x380, overlapping the IVT region)
and partly at FDC8 (HMA, above 1MB). The code at 0x0038 is dynamically generated by
DOSX during its init — it is NOT directly in DOSX.EXE's binary. FDC8 is DOSX's PM/RM
mode-switching trampoline that lives in the HMA.

PM detection mechanism (Windows KERNEL):
  The Windows kernel at 22C1:331D-332F tests IOPL settability to choose PM method:
    push 0xF000; push cs; call 0x3322; iret; pushf; pop ax; and ah,0xF0; je use_method_0
  If IRET can set IOPL (bits 12-13), the kernel uses GDT[0x0098]-based PM entry (method 0/1).
  If not settable, it falls back to INT 15h AH=87h (method 2), which doesn't set up the
  LDT properly, causing #NP at jmp far 0x009C:0x872F.

Fixes applied:
  - BIOS model byte 0xFF (IBM PC) → 0xFC (IBM AT/286): Windows machine-table lookup
    now succeeds; INT 15h AH=0xC0h returns correct model
  - dispatch_exception_pm IVT fallback removed: #GP with no IDT gate now escalates
    to #DF → triple fault instead of looping through the real-mode IVT
  - Triple fault warm-boot (corrected): BDA 0040:0069 = SS, 0040:0067 = SP (not CS:IP).
    IBM AT BIOS shutdown type 0x09 emulated as: load SS:SP, POP ES, POP DS, POPA, IRET.
    Stack layout at SS:SP=0x0DB5:0x00DE (from pm_entry_setup push sequence):
      [0x00DE] = 0x0DB5 (push ax #2 → popped to ES)
      [0x00E0] = 0x0DB5 (push ax #1 → popped to DS)
      [0x00E2..0x00F0] = PUSHA frame (DI,SI,BP,SP,BX,DX,CX,AX) → restored by POPA
      [0x00F2] = 0x0C13 (IP for IRET)
      [0x00F4] = 0x11E0 (CS for IRET = XMS segment in real mode)
      [0x00F6] = FLAGS
    After IRET: CS=0x11E0, IP=0x0C13 → resumes at real-mode 0x11E0:0x0C13.
  - FF/3 and FF/5 mod=11 panics replaced with #UD exception
  - LSL (0F 03) and LAR (0F 02) implemented: 0053:005A hits LSL AX,DX where
    DX=0x0098 (GDT entry for the zeroed segment). Required for setup to continue.
  - IRET on 286: mask changed from 0x0FFF to 0x7FFF, allowing IOPL bits 12-13 to be
    restored from the stack. This lets Windows KERNEL detect method 0/1 (GDT-based PM)
    instead of falling back to method 2 (INT 15h AH=87h). POPF kept at 0x0FFF mask to
    prevent HIMEM.SYS from misdetecting the CPU as 386+ and using 0x66 prefix instructions.

Current status:
  - jmp far 0x009C:0x872F (#NP) is now past; execution reaches DOSX.EXE real-mode code
  - FIXED: lbl_0038_630D port 0x2E36 busy-poll — McaStub now returns 0x00 (not busy).
    Port is a PS/2 system-board / MCA bus-arbitration register dynamically computed
    by DOSX init and stored at [DS:0x000A] (DS=0x0DB5, physical 0x0DB5A).
  - Next blocker unknown: after the poll loop exits, DOSX runs a 748-byte function
    at 0038:6314–0038:65FF that was never reached in the prior trace.

005B:0B61.label: pm_entry_setup
005B:0B61.comment:
Windows 3.x Standard Mode 286 protected-mode entry preparation.
Sets ES=0x004B; if setup stage < 3, pushes a real-mode IRET recovery frame
(FLAGS, XMS-seg, 0x0C13), saves own CS as warm-boot recovery segment,
writes warm-boot vector to BDA 0040:0067/0069, copies 6-byte GDT template
from 001B:03FA to ES:0x2A37, programs CMOS reg 0x0F=0x09 (shutdown via
warm-boot vector), saves PIC1 IMR, masks all IRQs, then calls far 00CB:0000.

00CB:0000.label: pm_entry_stub
00CB:0000.comment:
Real-mode stub that enters 286 protected mode.
Executes LGDT (GDT built at 000B:0058/0078 by the 005B:35xx preamble) then LMSW
to set the PE bit, switching the CPU to protected mode.
Far-jumps to CS=0x0078 (GDT entry 15, base=0x011E00 = XMS segment 0x11E0 × 16)
where the Windows PM kernel begins executing.
Not traced in execution log — execution resumes at 0078:xxxx in PM.

0255:03DC.label: a20_query_ps2
0255:03DC.comment:
Query current A20 gate state using the PS/2 fast gate method.
Returns AX=1 if A20 is enabled, AX=0 if disabled (or if PS/2 method is unavailable).
Checks [0x0031] first; if zero, skips the query entirely and returns 0.

0255:0418.label: a20_gate_ps2
0255:0418.comment:
Control or query the A20 gate via PS/2 fast gate (port 0x92, bit 1).
AX=0: disable A20 (clear bit 1); AX=1: enable A20 (set bit 1); AX>=2: query state.
After a set, polls port 0x92 up to 65536 times waiting for the bit to confirm.
Returns AX=1 on success or A20-enabled; AX=0 on timeout or A20-disabled.

0BFF:0147.label: vmm_dispatch_interrupt
0BFF:0147.comment:
Windows VMM cooperative interrupt dispatcher.
Acquires a test-and-set spinlock at ES:[BP] (vmm_ctrl_seg:vmm_ctrl_slot_ptr).
If the lock is free: saves current SS:SP into the control block, walks the linked
list of 8-byte slots to find the queued handler address, then simulates an interrupt
with pushf+call-far, restores SS:SP on return, releases the lock, and irets.
If the lock is busy (lbl_0BFF_01AF): yields without dispatching.

FDC8:5467.label: dosx_char_out_via_overlay
FDC8:5467.comment:
Output character AL via INT 29h if DOSX overlay 1 is loaded and ready.
Called ~1162 times from the character-routing loop at FDC8:550C.

Checks overlay 1 (BX=1) via dosx_get_overlay_entry:
  [SI+5] & 0x8080 == 0x0080  →  overlay loaded (bit 7) and not swapped (bit 15 clear)
  [SI+7] = far ptr to DOSX segment descriptor; [ptr+4] & 0x10 must be set
If both conditions hold: INT 29h (fast char output); CLC; ret.
Otherwise: falls through 91-byte load-overlay handler (FDC8:548D–54E7, never
executed in trace → overlay always ready) then RET at 54E8.

In recorded trace: [SI+5]=0x00D3 (bit 7 set) and [ptr+4]=0x13 (bit 4 set)
→ INT 29h fires all 1161 successful calls; one failure returns with carry set.

FDC8:54EB.label: dosx_write_char
FDC8:54EB.comment:
Character routing and output function (called per-character, ~1162 times).
Input: AL = character to write.

Printable path (AL in 0x20–0x7E):
  INC [SS:0x01F9]            ; bump column counter
  INC [SS:0x0300]; AND [...],0x3F   ; wrap output-pace counter mod 64
  If counter wrapped to 0: call func_FDC8_9137 (periodic scroll/flush check)
  CALL dosx_char_out_via_overlay   ; actual INT 29h output
  TEST [SS:0x02FE], 0xFF           ; check output-pending flag
  JE → 54E8 (ret)                  ; nothing pending → done

DEL (0x7F): treated like printable (skips INC [0x01F9]; goes to 54F8).

Control characters (AL < 0x20):
  0x0D (CR): MOV [0x01F9], 0x00 (clear column); jump to 54F8 for normal output path
  0x08 (BS): handled via lbl_FDC8_557A (not detailed in trace)
  0x09 (TAB): falls to 54F8 if not TAB

Called by dosx_write_char itself from FDC8:550C (within the printable path).
func_FDC8_9137 (periodic flush): calls dosx_get_overlay_entry(BX=0) and
func_FDC8_8413 with AH=1 (likely scroll or display refresh).

FDC8:7583.label: dosx_get_overlay_entry
FDC8:7583.comment:
SETUP.EXE DOSX overlay-table lookup (called ~1181 times).
Input: BX = overlay index (0 or 1 in recorded trace).
Output: SI = pointer to 59-byte overlay-info struct; carry set on error.

Two paths controlled by [SS:0x0357]:
  [0x0357] == 0 (always in trace): fast in-memory path.
    - Sets DS = SS (0x0116), saves ES and DI.
    - Calls dosx_find_overlay_by_index (A68A) which resolves:
        BX → 1-byte type code via type-table at ES:[0x0034]
        type code → 59-byte info entry at ES:[0x002A] + type*59 + 6
    - If A68A returns carry set: pop DI/ES and return error.
    - On success: restores DS = 0x0116; sets SI = DI (pointer to info entry); pop DI/ES; ret.
  [0x0357] != 0: slow disk-I/O path (FDC8:75A9, never taken in trace).
    Computes sector address from [0x05C2/0x05BE/0x05C0/0x05BC], calls
    dosx_disk_read (9511/94FA) in a loop, then dosx_write_sector_result (8D96).

Caller at FDC8:546B uses BX=1, checks [SI+5] bit 7 (loaded flag) and
[SI+7] far-pointer's +4 bit 4 before firing INT 29h (fast char output).

FDC8:A68A.label: dosx_find_overlay_by_index
FDC8:A68A.comment:
Resolve overlay index BX to a pointer to its 59-byte info struct.
Calls dosx_overlay_type_lookup (A66D) to validate BX and load:
  ES = segment containing the overlay tables
  DI = ES:[0x0034] + BX  →  1-byte type code for overlay BX
If A66D fails (carry set): go to A689 (les di,[0x0034]; add di,bx; ret).
Checks [ES:DI] == 0xFF → error (overlay slot empty): AL=6, stc, ret.
Otherwise: BL = [ES:DI] (type code); calls dosx_type_to_info_entry (A6A4)
which sets DI = ES:[0x002A] + BL*59 + 6 (pointer into 59-byte entry table).
Returns: DI = pointer to overlay info struct; carry clear on success.

FDC8:A66D.label: dosx_overlay_type_lookup
FDC8:A66D.comment:
Validate overlay index BX and return base pointer for type-code table.
Loads ES from [CS:0x3DE7] (= DOSX data segment 0x0116).
Loads ES from ES:[0x0330] (= segment selector for the overlay tables).
Compares BX against ES:[0x0032] (overlay count).
If BX >= count: stc; ret (error).
If BX < count:  DI = ES:[0x0034] + BX (pointer to 1-byte type code for BX);
                carry clear; ret.

FDC8:A6A4.label: dosx_type_to_info_entry
FDC8:A6A4.comment:
Convert type code in BX (low byte) to pointer to 59-byte overlay info entry.
Loads ES from [CS:0x3DE7] (= 0x0116).
LES DI, [ES:0x002A]  →  DI = base of 59-byte entry table.
Compares BX against [DI+4] (entry count).
If BX >= count: carry set (gap code not shown, goes to A6C2 path directly in trace).
At A6C2: DI += BX * 59 + 6  →  DI points to entry BX's data fields.
Each 59-byte entry at offset +0: type info; +4: flags (bit 4 = fault indicator);
+5: load flags (bit 7 = loaded into memory); +7: far ptr to DOSX segment descriptor.

0038:62F0.label: dosx_poll_device_ready
0038:62F0.comment:
DOSX real-mode function that polls a device status port until bit 7 (busy) clears.
Code at segment 0x0038 is dynamically generated by DOSX during init — not in DOSX.EXE binary.

Args (cdecl): [bp+0x04] = pointer to device descriptor struct in DOSX DS (0x0DB5).
               [bp+0x06] = status/flags word (not used in poll itself).
Struct layout at [DS:BX]:
  [BX+0x00]: flags word
  [BX+0x0A]: I/O port address to poll (e.g. 0x2E36)

Execution:
  Loads port from [BX+0x0A] into CX.
  Checks skip-poll flag at [DS:0x5E25]: if non-zero, takes 10-byte alternate path
  at 0038:6303-0038:630C (never executed in trace → flag always 0).
  Otherwise falls to lbl_0038_630D poll loop.
  After poll exits (bit 7 = 0): runs 748-byte function body at 0038:6314-0038:65FF
  (never executed in trace — stuck at poll).

Called from 0038:6608 (inside a larger block reached via FDC8:4237 IRET to 0038:6600).

FDC8:4419.label: dosx_device_check_stub
FDC8:4419.comment:
Stub device-check handler that unconditionally returns AL=0xFF ('not present/ready').
Called from the PM/RM switch dispatcher (FDC8:41xx chain) after parsing a device table
entry (type=0x03, values 0x08/0x03/0x02). The 0xFF return indicates the device
cannot provide status directly; DOSX falls back to polling the hardware port (0x2E36)
in real-mode after the IRET to 0038:6600.

0255:0429.label: a20_disable_path
0255:0429.comment: AX=0: clear AH so bit 1 will be left unset (disable A20), then fall into write path

0078:0C5D.label: pm_exit_sequence
0078:0C5D.comment: Intentional 286 PM exit: load null IDT then fire int3 to force triple fault → warm-boot

0038:630D.label: dosx_poll_busy_loop
0038:630D.comment:
Busy-wait loop: reads port 0x2E36, loops while bit 7 (busy) is set.
Port value comes from dosx_device_struct_port [DS:0x000A].
Fixed: McaStub returns 0x00 (not busy) — loop now exits on first read.

FDC8:4237.label: dosx_rm_trampoline_iret
FDC8:4237.comment:
IRET that returns from FDC8 PM/RM trampoline back to real-mode code.
Restores all registers from stack (AX=0x23FF, BX=0x00B1, CX=0x0023, DX=0x89E2,
SI=0x3FBF, DI=0x2E3A, BP=0x0038, DS=0x0DB5, ES=0x0DB5), then IRET to CS=0x0038
IP=0x6600. The IP (0x6600) was saved at FDC8:4225 from [0x05F2].

0038:6600.label: dosx_rm_after_trampoline
0038:6600.comment:
Entry point after FDC8 PM/RM trampoline IRET. Sets up args for dosx_poll_device_ready:
  shl [SI], 0x02   ; shift struct ptr (SI=0x3FBF, [DS:0x3FBF]=0 → still 0)
  add al, 0xd1     ; AL = 0xFF + 0xD1 = 0xD0 (status byte from preserved AX)
  push ax          ; arg2 = 0x23D0 (status)
  push [si]        ; arg1 = [DS:0x3FBF] = 0x0000 (device struct pointer)
  call dosx_poll_device_ready (0038:62F0)

lineComments.0255:0418: AX<2 = set operation (0=disable, 1=enable); AX>=2 = query current state
lineComments.0255:041B: preload enable bit mask; doubles as the target state value for polling after an enable
lineComments.0255:042F: merge desired state: AH=0x02 to enable, AH=0x00 to disable
lineComments.0255:0431: i/o delay — pipeline flush before reading back port 0x92
lineComments.0255:0437: CX=0 gives 65536 poll iterations on the first LOOPNZ decrement
lineComments.0255:0441: spin until A20 bit matches desired state or 65536 iterations elapsed
lineComments.0BFF:014A: load segment selector for the control block (GDT entry must cover offset 0x04CC+)
lineComments.0BFF:014F: load current control block slot offset within vmm_ctrl_seg
lineComments.0BFF:0156: test-and-set spinlock: atomically swap 1 in, read old value; 0 = lock was free
lineComments.0BFF:015E: advance control block list pointer by one 8-byte slot
lineComments.0BFF:0164: save current SP into control block for restoration after dispatch
lineComments.0BFF:0168: save current SS into control block (mov [bp+0x04], ss — disasm shows immediate)
lineComments.0BFF:016E: follow linked list: load next pending interrupt's control block offset
lineComments.0BFF:0172: sanity check: next slot's back-pointer must match current slot offset
lineComments.0BFF:018B: simulate an interrupt: push flags then far-call the handler
lineComments.0BFF:0192: reload control block pointer after handler return (ES:[SP] holds it)
lineComments.0BFF:019E: release spinlock: write 0 to ES:[BP] (lock byte)
lineComments.0BFF:01AB: discard the stale return address that was on the stack before the call into this function
lineComments.005B:0B66: check setup stage: ES:[0x0134] < 3 → first-time PM entry path
lineComments.005B:0B8B: check PM-already-entered flag: ES:[0x2F5A] == 0 means first entry
lineComments.005B:0B92: push real-mode IRET recovery frame: FLAGS, XMS segment (0x11E0), return IP (0x0C13)
lineComments.005B:0B9A: AX = CS:[0x0000] = own segment value (0x0DB5), used as warm-boot recovery CS
lineComments.005B:0B9E: if bit 1 of ES:[0x2F16] is clear, take PUSHA save-registers path
lineComments.005B:0BAC: 0x60 = PUSHA: save all general registers (AX CX DX BX SP BP SI DI)
lineComments.005B:0BDD: dest: ES:0x2A37 — GDT copy destination in config segment
lineComments.005B:0BE6: CMOS: disable NMI (bit 7) and select Shutdown Status register (0x0F)
lineComments.005B:0BF5: CMOS reg 0x0F = 0x09: shutdown type = jump via warm-boot vector at 0040:0067
lineComments.005B:0BF7: save current PIC1 IRQ mask before masking all hardware interrupts
lineComments.005B:0BFE: mask all hardware IRQs: PIC1 IMR = 0xFF
lineComments.005B:0C00: enter 286 protected mode via 00CB:0000 (MOVEPM / INT 15h AH=87h stub)
lineComments.005B:3591: 0x8CC8 = MOV AX,CS — load own segment value for GDT physical base calculation
lineComments.005B:3599: rep movsb: copy 0x35EC bytes from 005B:0000 → 004B:DI (relocate setup to XMS segment)
lineComments.005B:35A7: AX=0x11E0 × 0x10 → DX:AX=0x0001:0x1E00 — 24-bit physical base of XMS segment
lineComments.005B:35B8: far jump flushes instruction queue and reloads CS (required before GDT pointer writes)
lineComments.0078:0C5D: LIDT [null]: load IDT with base=0x000000, limit=0x0000 — any exception will triple-fault
lineComments.0078:0C62: int3 fires → #GP (IDT gate 3 beyond limit=0) → #DF → triple fault → BIOS warm-boot → BDA 0040:0067/0069
